texts.full_text: ℓ, corresponds to the selection of spherical harmonics and determines the number of planar nodes going through the nucleus (planar node is the midpoint between crest and trough with zero magnitude). The name "azimuthal quantum number" for ℓ was originally introduced by Sommerfeld, who refined Bohr's semi-classical model by replacing circular orbits with elliptic ones. The spherical orbitals were similar (in the lowest-energy state) to a rope oscillating in a large "horizontal" circle. See Azimuthal quantum number: history A: I believe this is what happened. Sommerfeld (1915, p. 430) $=$ (1916a, p. 12) originally introduced an “azimutal” quantum condition and number $n$ such that $\smash{\oint p_\varphi\,d\varphi=nh}$. This $n$ is what we now call the magnetic quantum number $m$. Then in (1916b) he implicitly switched his azimuth from being $\alpha$ to $\gamma$ in this figure, where the tilted plane would be his “Bahnebene”. As he writes, the resulting “azimutal quantum $n$ splits into quanta $n_1$ and $n_2$ belonging to the coordinates $\varphi$ and $\vartheta$”: $\smash{\oint p_\varphi\,d\varphi=n_1h}$ and $\smash{\oint p_\vartheta\,d\vartheta=n_2h}$. texts.full_text: specific immune cells (e.g., monocytes, neutrophils, and lymphocytes) in circulation and plasma levels of cytokines (e.g., TNF-*α* and IL-6) show \~24-hour daily rhythm that is translated into variations in acute response to infection \[[@B1], [@B2]\]. However, it remains poorly understood how such circadian immunity occurs. Recent studies reveal that the internal time-keeping system "circadian clock" is responsible for driving the circadian rhythms evident in the immune system. This review briefly summarizes new studies that provide mechanistic insights into how the immune system is under tight control of the circadian clock. In particular, this review puts emphases on a few recent reports revealing the critical role played by circadian clock proteins and their regulation mechanisms. 2\. Circadian clock {#sec2} ===================== The circadian clock is an endogenous time-keeping mechanism that enables organisms to adapt themselves to daily environmental changes caused by Earth's rotation \[[@B3]\]. The molecular basis of the circadian rhythm was first identified in and subsequently confirmed in other animals, including mammals. It is well established that a transcriptional-translational feedback loop (TTFL) involving clock genes such as , , , and underlies the molecular mechanism of circadian rhythm \[[@B4], [@B5]\]. The TTFL consists of positive and negative arms. In brief, CLOCK and BMAL1 proteins form heterodimers that bind to E-box sequences (CACGTG) in promoters of clock-controlled genes (CCGs), including and , thereby promoting their transcription \[[@B4], [@B5]\]. The PERIOD (PER) and CRYPTOCHROME (CRY) proteins, which are translated from transcribed mRNAs, form complexes that enter the nucleus to repress CLOCK-BMAL1-mediated gene expression. This process is further regulated by posttranslational modifications of clock components, such as phosphorylation \[[@B4], [@B5]\]. texts.full_text: id moor up for an hour at Tixall and had some lunch. I can imagine that a summers day it would be perfect to stop and have a nice walk and lunch at a pub on the way. The fridge was stocked with milk, plenty of tea and coffee (that wasn't decaf!)and there were enough mugs for all us plus extras! A large table meant we could sit down together as well which is if you are in lockdown bubble (if it comes to that again) or a family. There was even room at the other end of boat with another smaller circular sofa area, perfect fit an extra person/couple and somewhere else for them not be squashed up next two each! There were 2 Tv's on board which is great if you have younger children as they can plug in games consoles or just watch a film. They are mounted to the wall so it doesn’t matter how much rocking around there might happen, these screens aren‘ seem bothered by them at all and stay put! If I was going again (and that's probably unlikely given my propensity for being seasick on calm water) then i would definitely try an out of driving if possible. Maybe we should have brought some cards or a game to play instead, because once you get used the speed it’ very easy and there is no worry about getting into trouble

texts.full_text: there any equivalent java script function for ASP.Net method web.DownloadString ???? A: There's no such equivalent in javascript because of the Same Origin Policy restriction built in browsers which prevents you from downloading arbitrary files on remote domains. You could use AJAX to download resources but they must be hosted on the same domain as your page or have cross domain requests enabled. If this doesn't work for you, one possible solution is to write a server side script (could be ASP.NET) that will serve as bridge between javascript and the remote resource. For example: var url = 'http://www.google.com'; $.ajax({ type: 'GET', url: '/yourscript.aspx?url=' + encodeURIComponent(url), success: function(result) { // TODO: process the results } }); The /yourscript.aspx script would simply download the contents of the requested url and return it as a result. texts.full_text: traditional crystalline silicon (Si) wafer-based technologies by using significantly less semiconductor materials and offering a more cost effective solution for scale photovoltaic systems due to their potential lower production costs while also having good efficiency. Thin films can be made from various types of material such as amorphous Silicon, Cadmium Telluride (CdTe), Copper Indium Gallium Selenides and Peroskovites Plasma annealing is a technique used to improve the properties of thin film solar cells by exposing them to ionized gas or plasma. This process can enhance material quality through atomic rearrangement, reduce defects density such as dandling bonds in amorphous silicon based materials and also activate dopants thereby improving electrical conductivity while enhancing overall cell efficiency The present disclosure describes methods for performing a low-temperature annealing on thin film solar cells using plasma. The method includes placing the substrate with deposited semiconductor layers into vacuum chamber, filling process gas mixture to form treatment atmosphere and generating stable glow or pulsed DC/RF Plasma within certain frequency range depending upon desired outcome Moreover this technique can be easily integrated in Roll-to roll production line for flexible solar cells. This is a key advantage over other conventional annealing techniques like thermal which might not always suitable due to high temperature budget constraints and potential damage issues

texts.full_text: s the core mass spectrum in molecular clouds or the initial mass function of emerging stars - but also relate those through both pre- and protostellar stages. A number of the very basic questions of star formation still remain unanswered and debated (see, e.g., [@difrancescoppv], [@wardthompsonppv] and [@ballesterosppv] for recent reviews and discussions of both observational and theoretical studies), which can be addressed with large homogeneous surveys from mid-infrared through (sub)millimeter wavelengths and well-defined lists of sources such as those presented in this paper. For example, is the star formation process highly dynamical with cores being transient phenomena in a turbulent medium and are protostars only accreting in short periods of time when “passing by” these dense environments (i.e., Bondi-Hoyle accretion)? Or is low-mass star formation a more slowly evolving process as preferred in traditional ambipolar diffusion scenarios and in that case, how important are other effects such as turbulence in regulating the speed by which the process occurs? How efficient is the star formation process in turning prestellar dust and gas into young stars? Are there specific relationships between the natal prestellar cores and the emerging protostars, for example: do the properties of prestellar cores reflect in whether a given core actually forms a star and perhaps whether it forms a single or multiple system? Many of these discussions hinge on statistical arguments, for example estimates of the evolutionary rate of protostars through counts of the number of YSOs in different stages. Previous studies of the deeply embedded stages were hampered by low sensitivity single element (sub)millimeter receivers and confusion in low resolution, low sensitivity infrared observations such as those from the IRAS and ISO satellites. texts.full_text: looked at the sign in the butterfly pavilion as I always do, and silently thanked the Marshalls. And I thought about Jonathan, who died earlier this year. He was one of my earliest heroes, the owner and editor of the Scottsdale Daily Progress, my hometown newspaper. He was a liberal among conservatives, an opinionated guy. Wicked smart. By the time I finished grad school and applied for my first newspaper job, the Marshalls had sold the Progress — after that, it was never the paper it once was, suffering multiple big owners and eventually shutting its doors, gobbled by its parent company – but I was still proud to work there. And even prouder when I was invited to join a “multi-generational” book club that included Maxine among its members. We often met at the Marshalls’ home. Winters invites the viewer to marvel at the beautiful, yet fragile, Phoenix watershed, from its origins high in the Mogollon Rim to its destination in the Valley of the Sun. Winters says, "I believe people feel love and protectiveness for things they understand and find beautiful. This project intends to evoke that feeling toward the Phoenix watershed." "Winters is able to capture nature and the joy experienced, giving his viewers a real sense of this through his paintings", states Shade Projects Curator, Nicole Royse. The Closing Reception will be held on Third Friday, June 17th from 6-10pm featuring an intimate evening with the art and curator. Happily the answer is no. The first two episodes, which dropped yesterday on CBS and the network’s CBS All Access subscription service in the U.S.A. and on Netflix almost everywhere else today, are generally exciting and well-told, with high production values and a decent cast. However, at least with the evidence at hand, it does veer away from creator Gene Roddenberry’s hopeful spirit of exploration, and this may be an issue for some. Taking place some ten years or so before the original series’ tales of Captain Kirk and co (a point I’ll come back to later), Discovery features as its focus not the traditional Starfleet Captain (though there is one, played by Michelle Yeoh) but instead on the first officer of the USS Shenzhou, Michael Burnham, as portrayed by Sonequa Martin-Green. texts.full_text: Food Lab. It is a retrofitted bus that teaches children aboutsustainable food practices and healthy eating through hands-on cooking classes. The lab travels around the city, providing educational opportunities for students in various neighborhoods, irrespective of their socio-economic background. The Mobile Food Lab is a unique initiative by a group of educators and activists who believe in the power of education to create lasting change. By integrating practical cooking lessons with environmental awareness, they aim to address not only the health issues associated with poor dietary choices but also the environmental impacts of our food systems. The lab is equipped with all the necessary tools and appliances for cooking classes, including a professional kitchen setup. This allows students to learn culinary skills in a real-world setting, making the experience both fun and educational. The curriculum is designed to be age-appropriate and interactive, with lessons that cover topics such as food sourcing, plant-based diets, and the importance of reducing food waste. One of the key objectives of the Mobile Food Lab is to promote food literacy among childrEn. By understanding where their food comes from and how it is produced, students are encouraged to make healthier and more sustainable food choices. texts.full_text: a receiver that are strong enough to send and receive signals to and from a network base station, provided that the phone is near enough to the base station. Of course, this transmitter and receiver could in theory pick up the signals of other phones, but this is not something that is built into the Android operating system or any standard application. There are several reasons why this would be problematic: 1. Privacy Concerns: Privacy laws and regulations around the world are strict about the use of GPS tracking and other forms of surveillance. Collecting data on nearby devices without explicit user consent could violate these laws. 2. Technical Limitations: Android phones do not have built-in hardware that can broadcast or listen for signals from other phones' radios. The radio in your phone is designed to communicate with the network, not with other phones. 3. Security Risks: Allowing an app to scan for nearby phones and their unique IDs could pose significant security risks. This information could be exploited by malicious actors for identity theft or other forms of cybercrime. 4. texts.full_text: on next season’s squad. More than 1,000 fans attended the live event. The final team consists of women from Oklahoma, Texas, Utah, and as far away as Japan. This year’s team includes 12 veterans and 8 rookies. The audition process began last Saturday, on July 28, with more than 100 hopefuls. The process included preliminary tryouts, interviews and practices. Thirty-eight girls performed Thursday night for a spot on the team. Judges included Wayne Coyne, Miss Oklahoma 2012 Alicia Clifton, Joey and Heather from WILD 104.9 HD and Thunder officials. “This year’s talent and caliber exceeded my expectations,” said Thunder Girls manager and choreographer Paige Carter. “It was a difficult selection process for us to narrow it down to 20 members. I’m confident these ladies will uphold the standards and character it takes to be an ambassador for the Oklahoma City Thunder and the NBA.” The Thunder Girls make more than 200 appearances throughout the region for the Thunder at community and sponsorship events. The 2012-13 Thunder Girls are as follows (last names are not used so as to protect privacy): More than 100 talented ladies spent Saturday vying for spots in the Thunder Girls Finals, presented by Riverwind Casino. texts.full_text: non-intrusive method to determine bed levels in such a delayed coker drum. This method involves using at least one differential pressure (DP) transmitter installed along the height of the coke drum. The DP transmitter measures the differential pressure between two points separated by a known distance. By installing multiple DP transmitters at different heights, the bed level can be inferred based on the changes in measured DP. When the bed level reaches the sensing point of a DP transmitter, the DP reading changes abruptly due to the phase change from vapor to liquid or solid. These abrupt changes indicate the bed level at that specific point. This non-intrusive method provides several advantages over traditional intrusive methods such as probes and sight glasses. It eliminates the risk of equipment damage due to physical intrusion into the process and reduces maintenance requirements. Moreover, it can provide continuous real-time monitoring, allowing operators to make informed decisions on switching cycles between drums to optimize production. In one embodiment, the method comprises: 1. Installing at least two DP transmitters at different heights

texts.full_text: (LEGH) is a benign proliferative disease of cervical glands. Although histological resemblance of minimal deviation adenocarcinoma (MDA) to LEGH and frequent association of LEGH with MDA have been reported, it still remains unclear whether LEGH is a precancerous lesion of MDA. The present study was undertaken to examine the pathogesis behind this transition from non-neoplastic hyperplasia (LEGH) to malignant adenocarcinoma (MDA). To explore these changes, we performed clonality analysis using X-chromosome inactivation patterns and allelic imbalance studies on a series of LEGH and MDA samples. These techniques allow us to assess whether the proliferative cells are monoclonal or polyclonal originating from one precursor cell (monoclonal) or multiple precursors respectively. In our analysis, we found that most LGEH cases showed no evidence for clonality suggesting a non-neoplastic nature of LEGH. However, someLGEH and all MDA samples exhibited monoclonal patterns indicating neoplasia - implying an ongoing process where initially polyclonal hyperplastic glands progress into single-cell-derived malignant tumors. The transition from LGEH to MDA was further supported by the identification of STK11 gene mutations in a subset of LEGH cases that showed monoclonal patterns, suggesting these genetic changes could be driving forces for this progression. This evidence is consistent with prior studies which have shown frequent co-occurrence and genomic similarities betweenLEGHandMDA. Our findings add to the growing understanding about LGEH's potential role as a precursor lesion

texts.full_text: te - tbh http://blog.metricfire.com/2012/03/paul-graham-consistently-says-um-7-times-per-minute/ ====== simonsarris If anyone has a similar habit and wants to stop it I will tell how my high school fixed it for almost all of us. In my (private) high school's health class "like" and "um" and others were referred to as "stop-words" by the teacher because people would say them instead of pausing. It's really obvious once you look for them, for instance here with pg but anywhere really. I remember hearing college tour guides that would literally say "um" after every single sentence, probably unbeknownst to themselves! Almost every class in the school had projects, and the health class project was for us to remove the stop words from our speech by the end of the semester. We did this by all using recording software (had to submit either by cassette tape or wav/mp3) and answering questions such as "Do you want to live forever and why or why not?" by speaking for at least 5 minutes. These were our homework assignments maybe once a month, with the overarching goal considered as the class project. We had to very consciously never use any stop words. We could pause the recorder if we had trouble thinking of what to say, but we could never say those words. I was skeptical of the assignment at first but my classmates and I found that by the end it became much easier not just for us personally in our speech habits overall because this practice made you aware when those

y.a). Shoulder arthroscopy is getting more and more popular among orthopaedic community. Efficacy of arthroscopic treatment of shoulder instability approaches that of conventional operative techniques.
Aim. The purpose of the study was the analysis of arthroscopic treatment of traumatic shoulder instability.
Material and methods. Study was based on 22 patients, 24 shoulders were operated. Capsulolabral complex has been stabilized arthroscopically in all patients.
Functional was assessed by means of ASES, UCLA and SST scores. Function was improved significantly. There hThere have been significant improvements in functional outcomes as measured by the American Shoulder and Elbow Surgeons (ASES) score, University of California at Los Angeles (UCLA), and Subjective Shoulder Value (SSV) or Simple Shoulder Test (SST). These scores reflect subjective patient satisfaction and objective measures of shoulder function such as range of motion, strength, pain levels, and overall activity level. The study aimed to evaluate the efficacy of arthroscopic stabilization in treating traumatic shoulder instability. All 24 shoulders underwent an arthroscopic procedure focusing on stabilizing the capsulolabral complex which is crucial for maintaining shoulder stability after dislocation or subluxation events. Postoperative follow-up assessments revealed substantial enhancements across all functional scales, indicating that patients experienced reduced pain and improved ability to perform

Function: These are transcription factors regulating gene expression by binding DNA, crucial for processes like plant growth regulation. - Two types exist: those with two AP2 domains (e.g., Apetela2) involved in floral patterning. - Those with a single domain known as ERFs linked to ethylene responses and stress management under abiotic stresses such as drought or cold. Examples of their role can be found by researching studies on plant stress response mechanisms. 2. Evolutionary Distribution: Canonical AP2 domains are prevalent in land plants, but divergent homologues exist elsewhere (cyanobacteria, ciliates). This suggests an ancient origin and potential horizontal gene transfer from bacteria to early eukarya. 3. Structural Implications: - Two-domain proteins may offer enhanced versatility or precision due to combined signals. - Single domains might be simpler but still versatile in targeting different DNA sequences, possibly leading to varied functions based on domain variations and composition differences that affect binding affinity for specific motifs. 4. This influence is evident in his work that often features cars and motorcycles as central subjects, which are depicted with a vibrant color palette and dynamic compositions typical of Pop Art style but adapted to Scandinavian aesthetics. Franzén's most notable works include large-scale paintings of classic automobiles like the Chevrolet Bel Air and Harley-Davidson motorcycles. His art captures not only the aesthetic appeal of these vehicles but also their cultural significance, especially within American society during different decades. This has made his work popular among car enthusiasts as well as collectors interested in contemporary Scandinavian Pop Art. Beyond his automotive-themed paintings, Franzén is recognized for creating portraits and other artwork commissioned by official institutions such as Sweden's postal service (Posten) and the Royal Court of Sweden. texts.full_text: larming are headaches that occur when someone changes position,” says Dr. De Los Rios. “For example, you may get a headache when you lay down, but not when you’re upright.” Migraines occur less often than tension-type headaches, but they can be much more severe and debilitating. They are six times more common in women than men. Migraines are commonly diagnosed as chronic if they occur over at least 15 days per month for more than 3 months. Although a migraine can hit without warning, it is often set off by a trigger. The triggers that set off a migraine vary, but a migraine sufferer usually remains sensitive to the same triggers. Those triggers can include certain odors, fasting, changing weather, lack of sleep, fatigue, stress, loud noises, wine, cheese and other foods. When to Seek Emergency HelpIf a headache doesn’t seem routine at all and comes on suddenly and overwhelmingly, then you may need to call 911. Unruptured brain aneurysms are often asymptomatic. These aneurysms are typically small in size, usually less than one half inch in diameter. However, large unruptured aneurysms can occasionally press on the brain or nearby nerves. Any individual experiencing some or all of the following symptoms, regardless of age, should undergo immediate and careful evaluation by a physician. New severe, worsening or persistent headache. Sudden onset of very severe headache over a few minutes. Confusion. Headaches that are occurring more and more frequently. Headache that worsens with changes in head position (sitting up or lying down). Headache that wakes you up from sleep. If the headache is associated with nausea and vomiting upon awakening. The theorem states that if a function is continuous on a closed interval and differentiable on an open interval, then there exists a point in the open interval where the derivative is equal to zero. Now, to answer your question, yes, you are on the right track. The statement you provided is a correct proof of the Rolle's Theorem, and it is a good start. However, there are a few minor issues with your understanding of the proof. Here's a brief outline of the proof: 1. The function $f$ is continuous on the closed interval $[a,b]$, and differentiable on the open interval $(a,b)$. 2. The statement assumes that there exists a point $c \in (a,b)$ such that $\frac{f(b)-f(c)}{f(c)-f(a)} < 0$. 3. This inequality can be rewritten as $\frac{f(b)-f(c)}{f(c)-f(a)} < 0$. 4. Using the definition of the derivative, we can rewrite this inequality as $\frac{f'(c)}{f(c)} < 0$. 5. Since $f$ is differentiable on $(a,b)$, we know that $f'(c)$ exists for all $c \in (a,b)$. 6.
